item.text: {"outcome":"fail","confidence":"Firm","findings":[{"check":"A-SEC-6","title":"Reset tokens are generated with predictable non-CSPRNG values","severity":"High","path":"src/reset.js","line":6,"quote":"const token = `${Date.now().toString(36)}${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;"},{"check":"A-SEC-6","title":"Reset-token verification does not consume the token","severity":"Medium","path":"src/reset.js","line":10,"quote":"used: false"}],"reasoning":"bcrypt uses cost 12 and bcrypt.compare; the reset limiter is mounted before its route; reset responses are generic; and no development bypass exists. However, reset token generation uses Date.now and Math.random instead of a CSPRNG. The repository contains no mutation that changes record.used after successful verification, so issued reset tokens are not established as single-use."}
